description_long: This is a compact, vintage-style electric radiator with a rectangular, upright form and a predominantly red metal body. The front features a large black grille with closely spaced horizontal and vertical bars, behind which the heating elements are visible. The top section houses a control panel with a rotary knob and a small display, both set in a beige frame, and a power cord extends from the rear. The sides and back are solid red metal, with the back panel featuring ventilation slots and a label. The top and bottom edges are reinforced with black trim, giving the unit a sturdy appearance. The surface shows signs of wear, suggesting it has been used in urban or industrial environments.
description: A vintage red electric radiator with a black grille front, rotary control knob, and visible power cord, showing signs of wear and urban use.
description_view_0: Front-left view showing the black grille, control panel, and the start of the power cord on the side.
description_view_1: Rear-left view highlighting the solid red metal back, ventilation slots, and the full length of the power cord.
description_view_2: Direct rear view focusing on the back panel's ventilation slots and the cord emerging from the upper section.
description_view_3: Front-right view emphasizing the grille, control knob, and the side panel with a visible switch or vent.
attribute_pipe_connections: none visible; power cord at rear
attribute_grille_pattern: rectangular grid of horizontal and vertical bars
